description.en: A crisp, spiced flatbread from Levantine and Egyptian kitchens, its dough rubbed with olive oil and studded with mahlab, anise, fennel and sesame. Rolled thin and baked at high heat until deeply golden, it keeps for days as a dunking bread for tea or coffee.
description.de: Ein knuspriges Gewürzfladenbrot aus der levantinischen und ägyptischen Küche, der Teig mit Olivenöl vermengt und mit Mahlab, Anis, Fenchel und Sesam gespickt. Dünn ausgerollt und heiß gebacken bis tief golden, hält es sich tagelang zum Eintunken in Tee oder Kaffee.
description.es: Un pan plano y crujiente con especias de la cocina levantina y egipcia, con masa mezclada con aceite de oliva y sazonada con mahlab, anís, hinojo y sésamo. Se estira fino y se hornea a fuego fuerte hasta dorarse, y aguanta días para mojar en té o café.
description.fr: Un pain plat croustillant et épicé du Levant et d'Égypte, la pâte à l'huile d'olive parfumée au mahlab, à l'anis, au fenouil et au sésame. Étalée fine et cuite à four très chaud, elle se garde des jours pour tremper dans le thé ou le café.

origin.note: Mahlab, ground from St. Lucie cherry pits, flavors sweet breads and pastries across the Eastern Mediterranean; this seeded, olive-oil version is a home-bakery teatime bread rather than a festival loaf.

ingredients.note: rubbed into the flour until sandy, plus extra for brushing
not specified by source; a modest amount added as is typical for yeasted dough
white and optional black sesame, for the dough and topping

keeping.note: Its whole point is long shelf life: keeps over a week in an airtight tin and needs no reheating.

method.title: Bloom yeast
method.summary: Dissolve the sugar and yeast in part of the water; let stand until foamy.

method.title: Mix dry ingredients
method.summary: Combine flour, milk powder, anise, sesame, mahlab and fennel, then rub in the olive oil until the mixture looks like wet sand.

method.title: Knead
method.summary: Add the yeast mixture and the remaining water; knead into a smooth, firm dough.

method.title: Rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough double in size, about 1 hour.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Divide into pieces and roll each into a thin round, about 1 cm thick or thinner for a crisper result.

method.title: Top
method.summary: Arrange on an oiled tray, brush with olive oil, and scatter over more sesame seeds.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake in a very hot oven until the undersides are deep gold, then broil briefly to color the tops.

source.adaptation: Cup measures converted to grams; source did not list salt, so a modest typical amount was added; chose the 10-large-piece option over the 20-small alternative.
